category_title: Visual Learner Struggling with Traditional Research
minimal_context: The student is in Spanish class and is expected to make a presentation in front of the class to show their knowledge.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We're making presentations today to show our knowledge in Spanish class. It is your turn to present.

STUDENT: I don't want to go up there.

TUTOR: Please walk to the front of the room to begin.

STUDENT: No, I really don't want to go up there in front of the class.

TUTOR: You need to do your presentation now.

STUDENT: I can't. I don't want to go up there by myself.

options.A: Offer to go up to the front of the room with the student while they give their presentation.
options.B: Allow the student to withdraw from the classroom for a time-limited period to prevent an emotional overload.
options.C: Allow the student to not be rushed, giving them space to stop, think, and start the presentation again.
options.D: Remind the student of deadlines, stating that they must complete their Spanish presentation by the end of class.
